gaps: User-scope sync resources: The default sync set covers app-token-viable resources (games-top, streams, content-classification-labels, chat-emotes-global, eventsub-subscriptions). Resources requiring a user OAuth token or a broadcaster_id/user_id parameter (clips, videos, users, followers, analytics reports) stay reachable via --resources but are not synced by default.

Search live fallback: search resolves against the local synced corpus; with an empty store it falls back to a live category lookup rather than failing, but rich cross-resource search requires a prior sync.

novel_features.name: Local SQLite Sync
novel_features.description: Mirror Twitch Helix data (top games, live streams, global emotes, content labels) into a local SQLite store for offline search and analysis
novel_features.rationale: No Twitch tool persists data; every library and the official twitch-cli is a stateless live passthrough that forgets each response

novel_features.name: Full-Text Search
novel_features.description: Full-text search across locally synced Twitch records
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local SQLite index; impossible with the stateless API wrappers (nicklaw5/helix, twurple, TwitchIO) that hold no durable corpus

novel_features.name: Local Analytics
novel_features.description: Group-by and count aggregation over locally synced Twitch data without re-hitting the rate-limited API
novel_features.rationale: Offline aggregation is absent from every alternative; consumers of the existing libraries must hand-roll it against live responses

novel_features.name: Compound Workflows
novel_features.description: Chain multiple Helix operations into one agent-friendly command
novel_features.rationale: Combines several API calls behind a single invocation; the official twitch-cli api command is a one-shot passthrough with no composition
